verdict_reason: The verse refuses the easy reading (anti-metric coaching content) and holds the harder, more precise diagnostic — the implicit closure clause. The transliterated technical terms are preserved load-bearing, not erased. The original meaning is preserved alongside in literal_meaning and traditional_meaning_consensus. The doctrinal scoping (Shankara: kāmya-karma portion specifically; Ramanuja: attachment-driven partial reading specifically; not the Vedas as such) is preserved with care. The verse does not claim the Sanskrit is 'really about' standing-review hygiene; it offers the standing-review case as one operational analog. No trivialisation.
